title: batrachium foeniculaceum
intro: Batrachium foeniculaceum, also known as hornwort, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Batrachospermales. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also believed to have health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Batrachium foeniculaceum can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and are often used to create teas, salves, and poultices for topical application.
